headline: The recommended channels converge on agents reshaping the development stack, while open weights and compiler rewrites push raw capability higher.
body: IBM Technology argues AI speeds coding but leaves productivity gains limited unless SDLC workflows are redesigned around AI agents for testing and delivery [2], a thread Cursor extends at Compile 26 where Michael Truell reports 95% of usage is now agents and announces Cursor Mobile, the Origin Git platform, and a from-scratch model with dedicated agent compute [13]. On capability, Better Stack covers GLM-5.2, an MIT-licensed open-weights model that tops the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and beats GPT-4.5 on GDPval [6], plus TypeScript 7 rewritten from TypeScript into Go for roughly 10x faster type checking with shared-memory parallelism [5]. Julia Turc dissects Mistral's Voxtral Realtime and Voxtral TTS, walking through modular versus end-to-end speech design and audio tokenization via vector and finite scalar quantization against milestones like Whisper and WaveNet [1]. IBM rounds out the cycle with data resiliency fundamentals: the 3-2-1 backup rule and risk-balanced security spending [0][4].

cited_items.title: AI in the SDLC: Rethinking AI Coding Tools & AI Agents
GLM 5.2 is my new favorite model...
